mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and reduces the attractiveness of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.3 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 103 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces economic growth and decreases equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and reduces investor sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 2.7 billion signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts economic growth and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that compresses unemployment rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2.2 trillion signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods and services that boosts economic growth and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
